forced_causal_chains.topic: sulphites (SO₂) and consequences of excessive use

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Sulphur dioxide, or SO₂, is used as an antioxidant and antimicrobial preservative in winemaking.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: When used excessively, SO₂ can suppress microbial and fermentative activity too broadly and leave perceptible residual sulphur characters in the wine.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The wine can show reductive aromas such as struck match, burnt rubber, or sulphur, and its fruit character can seem masked.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Excess sulphites can harm wine character because too much SO₂ suppresses fermentative expression and can leave reductive aromas; precise dosing is therefore needed to preserve the wine without masking it.
